prevalence.source: Academia Brasileira de Neurologia + MS epidemiology syntheses
prevalence.trend: increasing due to improved diagnosis and survival

riskFactors: low vitamin D/limited sun exposure patterns
vulnerablePopulations: patients in Southern Brazil (higher latitude association in some datasets)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: McDonald criteria with dissemination in time and space (MRI) and CSF oligoclonal bands as supportive; clinical relapses and neurologic exam

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; improved with disease-modifying therapy access and early diagnosis

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: MRI and neurology access concentrated in larger centers; diagnosis delays in remote areas
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Disease-modifying therapies via SUS specialized components in many settings; relapse treatment with corticosteroids; rehabilitation services
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Unequal access to neurologists/MRI and timely escalation to high-efficacy DMTs
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Specialized MS care exists in major centers and public provision of several DMTs
